name: Bemdaneprocel showed graft-survival and exploratory motor signals in Parkinson disease
summary: A phase 1 Parkinson disease trial reported safety, PET evidence consistent with graft survival, and exploratory motor improvement after hES-derived dopaminergic progenitor transplantation.
statement: In 12 people with Parkinson disease, bilateral putamen grafting of hES-derived dopaminergic neuron progenitors met phase 1 safety and tolerability objectives, showed increased putaminal 18F-DOPA PET uptake at 18 months, and had exploratory motor improvement or stability.
population_or_model: People with Parkinson disease enrolled in an open-label dose-escalation phase 1 trial
quantitative_note: The high-dose cohort had an average 23-point improvement in MDS-UPDRS Part III OFF scores, with no graft-induced dyskinesias reported.
caveats: The trial was open-label, small, and primarily designed for safety and tolerability.
All participants received immunosuppression, and stereotactic grafting is invasive.
Parkinson disease replacement therapy does not establish broad reversal of stem-cell exhaustion or healthy-aging benefit.
